component.name: SteelVoiceMix
component.summary: ChatMix for SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless on Linux

description.p: SteelVoiceMix brings ChatMix functionality to Linux for the SteelSeries Arctis Nova Pro Wireless headset. It creates virtual PipeWire audio sinks (Game and Chat) that are controlled by the hardware dial on the base station, providing native Linux support for the headset's ChatMix feature.

ul.li: Hardware ChatMix dial controls Game/Chat audio balance
Optional GUI with real-time volume bars and battery indicator
Desktop notifications on connect/disconnect
Auto-reconnect when device sleeps or is unplugged
Follows KDE/GNOME system theme

screenshot.caption: Main window — connection status, Game/Chat volumes, battery, and overlay settings
Dial overlay — vertical style, flashes briefly when you turn the hardware dial

description.p: Major release: Volume Boost (per-channel 100-200% digital amplification with clipping warning + pre-boost dial snapshot/restore). Multi-language UI with Arabic + RTL support. Default-sink cycle keyboard shortcut and CLI.

Channel-aware update checker (dev users see dev, stable users see stable). APP_VERSION derived from RPM at runtime. Chatmix dial position persisted across daemon restarts. Auto Game-EQ snapshot persisted so close-game-then-suspend no longer leaks the game preset into the user's default.

Audio glitch fixes: filter chains set node.lock-quantum, node.suspend-on-idle = false, node.latency = 1024/48000, and the surround convolver pins blocksize = 512. Defends against PipeWire issue #4013 / EasyEffects #1567 false- resync glitches during fast scene transitions and alt-tab refocus.

Major release: full mic-side processing chain (Noise Gate, Noise Reduction, AI Noise Cancellation, Volume Stabilizer) plus per-channel mic EQ with 14 bundled ASM presets. New Auto Game-EQ feature applies a matching preset to the Game channel based on the running game's audio client; supports drag-orderable manual bindings.

Audio profiles now snapshot EQ + mic state. Voice-test loopback for A/B'ing changes. Theme switcher (Auto / Light / Dark) with system-following default. Responsive window. Crash fix on systemd restart. Logging quieter at INFO; full trail via DEBUG. Mic features and sidetone tagged ALPHA.
Patch release: three small UX fixes on top of v0.3.0.

ul.li: 'Check for updates' button now bypasses the 24-hour cache so it actually checks GitHub each time you click it.

EQ slider labels fall back to 'Band 1..10' when a parametric preset clusters multiple sliders in the same musical range (ASM game-specific tunings often do this — three sliders all labelled 'Brilliance' was confusing).
HDMI sink toggle in the Sinks tab gets an ALPHA badge to signal it hasn't been hardware-verified against a real TV / AVR yet.

description.p: Big release. Two major features land alongside a complete GUI overhaul.

ul.li: Per-channel tunings for Game, Chat, Media, and HDMI sinks via PipeWire filter chains.
Searchable preset library — 391 ASM-curated game/chat presets bundled with the package.
Up to 5 favourites per channel pinned to a quick-access bar above the sliders.
Auto-forks to a Custom-N user preset when you start editing a built-in.
Built-in test audio (pink noise, sweeps, tones) for ear-checking the chain.
SteelSurround 7.1 sink + HRIR convolver chain over the headset.
Generic HeSuVi-format reference HRIR bundled (`EAC_Default.wav`); Browse for a personalised one any time.
On by default; routes Game / Chat / Media through the convolver. HDMI bypasses (TV / AVR handles surround natively).
Left sidebar navigation replaces top-tab strip.
Card-style section grouping across every tab.
Animated iOS-style toggle widgets where on/off.
Coloured status pill in the header.
JS-style searchable select for the EQ preset picker (mouse-wheel ignored).
New Microphone tab placeholder for upcoming noise-gate / NR / AI-NC features.
Reset-to-defaults button in Settings (preserves saved audio profiles).
Surround channel mapping bug that caused right-channel audio to bleed into the left ear.
Dial overlay no longer appears in the KDE Plasma 6 taskbar.
Minimize-to-tray toast is now opt-in.
Test-audio clips have a 200 ms fade-in and conservative reference levels.

description.p: Fixes the dial overlay to always show on the primary monitor instead of following the mouse cursor, which behaved unpredictably on multi-display setups. Also modernizes the release pipeline: GitHub Actions triggers COPR builds on tag push, the spec uses rpkg macros for resilient SRPM generation, and Dependabot patch/minor bumps auto-merge on green CI. Minor dependency updates and a dead-code cleanup.

Virtual sinks renamed to Steel* (SteelGame, SteelChat, SteelMedia). Adds a new SteelMedia sink that bypasses the ChatMix dial, runtime-toggleable from the GUI. Fixes a bug where the GUI's Add/Remove Media buttons did nothing. install.sh grew a --no-gui flag for headless setups.

Daemon rewritten in Rust for performance and reliability. GUI now talks to the daemon over a Unix socket. OLED base-station display shows a live ChatMix gauge. Includes exponential-backoff reconnection and a volume-control bugfix.
Initial release with ChatMix support, GUI monitor, and battery indicator.
